Question Answers Notes Marks Criterion
1 a 50 (km h-1) 1 A

 head wind
 drag
2 A
 different weather systems such as rain
 increasing fatigue or other physiological feature as they fly
 behavioural flight patterns eg flying in groups
c diagram 4
1 A

between points C and D the retarding force is increasing or forces are no longer balanced
speed:
reducing to zero ECF from 1st marking point for marking
points 2, 3 and 4 4 C
acceleration:
deceleration / retardation / negative acceleration
Newton’s law:
second law or quoting F=ma
e (20*120*0.5) or 1200 or 1.2
2 C
m or km Unit to agree with numerical value

any two labels correct 2 A
all labels correct
b 12 (Hz) 1 C

330 Unit required for 2nd mark 2 C
(wavelength = =) 55 m Award one mark only for correct
6
wavelength without unit
e wave D 1 C
f identification of diffraction
general explanation of diffraction
3 A
how diffraction relates to the specific situation
3 a
2 A
air correctly identified
water and glass correctly identified
b light refracts or changes direction or bends (when it changes medium)
degree to which it refracts is dependent on change in density of the medium WTTE but there must be an implied
or change in medium for marking point 2
degree to which it refracts is dependent on the relative change in the refractive index of
the materials 3 A
or
degree to which it refracts is dependent on the change in its speed
(because) the ray bends towards the normal as it slows down ORA
4 a
Charge
Electron -1
1 A
Proton +1
Neutron 0
b electrons are the only particle which can move
electrons are transferred Accept “negative charge in place of 3 A
electron” for marks 2 and 3
(so) a lack of electrons creates a positive charge
c cat is charged by rubbing the carpet
pellets are uncharged
reference to the charge on the cat inducing or causing or creating an opposite charge in
4 A
the pellets
the charge on the cat is attracted to the electrons/protons/opposite charges in the pellets
or
the pellets are then attracted to the cat
5 a conduction
convection 3 A
radiation
b any reasonable suggestion of equipment, for example Do not accept ice or paper
 timer
 balance 2 B
correctly linked justification
c statement of prediction that links number of sheets of paper and mass of ice that
has melted, for example
mass of ice melted decreases as number of sheets of paper increases
2 B
correctly linked justification, for example
number of sheets of paper is increasing gives more insulation which reduces mass of ice
that melts
d statement of reasonable variable
statement of how the variable should be controlled 3 B
justification of why the variable should be controlled
e indication of how mass lost will be determined
collect at least five sets of data 3 B
repeat each set at least three times and average
6 a (not supported because) as the thickness increases the time taken to cool down follows
no trend
or 1 C
2 mm coffee cup was the slowest to cool down
b Four weaknesses and correctly linked scientific explanation of the effect of each Accept answers relating to number of
weakness, for example trials even though this is a procedural
different thicknesses of cup were used weakness
different materials were used which will have different thermal conductivities
different coloured materials were used
different coloured materials will radiate thermal energy / heat differently
volume was not the same each time 8 C
filling to the same height does not give the same volume as the dimensions are different
the cups were of different dimensions
the surface area exposed to the air was different for each cup
the room temperature was not measured
the room temperature may not have been controlled or could have varied
7 a IV – thickness of aluminium sheet
2 B
DV – amount / intensity of beta radiation absorbed or that penetrates
b electron 2
A
(that is) high-energy or fast-moving
c as the thickness of the aluminium sheet increases the intensity of the beta radiation that ORA
penetrates the sheet decreases
2 B
as the aluminium sheet is made thicker the electrons would have less chance of
penetrating due to more energy loss or increased interaction or collisions with atoms
d Four points describing the method given below
 at least five conditions of the independent variable
 measurements repeated at least three times or counts averaged
 count rate measured with no aluminium between source and GM tube
 measurements taken with aluminium sheets of increasing number between source and 6
GM tube B
Any two further reasonable points, for example
 control variable e.g. distance/orientation between source and GM tube should be fixed
 aluminium sheets of the same thickness
 same source should be used for each test
e Any reasonable source of background radiation, for example
 cosmic rays
1 A
 rocks
 fallout from nuclear testing
f background radiation should be measured without the presence of the beta source
2 B
background count should be deducted from all future measurements
g any two reasonable precautions and correctly related reason, for example
Precaution Reason
minimize time of exposure reduce dose because exposure~time
increase distance between
source and person or use reduce dose because exposure~1/distance
tongs 4 B
reduce dose because radiation is absorbed by Do not accept gloves or goggles
shielding/protective clothing
shielding
avoid pregnant women reduce risk of genetic abnormality
do not point source towards
reduce dose because β radiation is directional
body
h hypothesis
alpha particles would be stopped completely by the first aluminium sheet
explanation 2 B
alpha particles are less penetrating than beta particles
or
alpha particles have higher mass and have more interaction with matter than beta
8 a six times identified
values ranging between 0 and 100
equally spaced values
6 C
two averages calculated correctly
all correct
averages expressed as whole numbers Award the sig fig marks for any
number correctly expressed
b smooth curve 1 C
c one half life correctly read from graph
two further half lives calculated 3 C
correct calculation of average
d 4 half lives Award only one mark for four years
2 A
8 years

 large lorries delivering coal

or

increasing the voltage reduces the current
3 D
less heat is generated (so less energy is lost)

water vapour
greenhouse gas

in order to encourage people to use this energy it is sold more cheaply
10 a any reasonable application requiring a constant source of electricity
1 D
b
 incomplete statement of reason for constant source of
electricity with no reference to application in 10a 1–2
 advantage or disadvantage stated with no reference to 10a
 reason for a constant source of electricity related to
application in 10a is stated
3–5
